expectations.text: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
expectations.evidence: Section 1 labeled 'PDB (Pod Disruption Budget) Blocking Drain — Most Common Cause' and listed first in diagnostic order.

expectations.text: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
expectations.evidence: Includes 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' with guidance to look for ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS = 0.

expectations.text: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
expectations.evidence: Multiple diagnostic commands: bare pod detection via jq, pending pods check, FailedScheduling events, termination grace period audit.

expectations.text: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 covers Pending pods, FailedScheduling events, kubectl top nodes, and allocated vs available resources.

expectations.text: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
expectations.evidence: Two options: patch PDB with maxUnavailable 100%, or backup-edit-apply workflow. Includes restore-after-upgrade reminder.

expectations.text: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
expectations.evidence: Section 2 fix shows gcloud command to increase maxSurge to 3 with explanation: 'allows up to 3 temporary extra nodes during upgrade.'

expectations.text: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
expectations.evidence: Section 4 covers admission webhooks rejecting pod creation, with commands to list and describe webhook configurations, and fix to temporarily delete.

expectations.text: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Validation After Applying a Fix' section with watch commands for node progress, pod status, and gcloud operations list.
